# 04 â€” The default `Jido` instance is referenced everywhere but never started

**Severity:** High (first-contact experience)
**Location:** `lib/jido/application.ex`, `lib/jido/agent_server.ex` (`start/1`, moduledoc examples), `lib/jido/agent_server/options.ex` (`normalize_attrs/2`), `lib/jido/agent_server/directive_executors.ex` (Emit/Spawn fallbacks)

## Problem

`Jido.Application.start/2` starts only `Jido.Storage.ETS.Owner`. Yet the codebase defaults
to process names derived from a Jido instance literally named `Jido`:

- `Options.normalize_attrs/2`: `jido_instance = Map.get(attrs, :jido, Jido)`,
  `registry = Jido.registry_name(jido_instance)` â†’ **`Jido.Registry`**
- `AgentServer.start/1`: `nil -> Jido.AgentSupervisor`
- Emit executor fallback: `Jido.TaskSupervisor`
- Spawn executor fallback: `Jido.AgentSupervisor`

None of these processes exist unless the user happens to start `{Jido, name: Jido}` â€”
which no documentation suggests. The documented "default instance" (`Jido.start/0`) is
named **`Jido.Default`**, whose registry is `Jido.Default.Registry`, so it doesn't satisfy
these fallbacks either.

Consequence: the *first example in the AgentServer moduledoc* â€”
`{:ok, pid} = AgentServer.start_link(agent: SimpleAgent)` â€” crashes in `init/1` when
`Registry.register(Jido.Registry, ...)` raises `ArgumentError` (unknown registry), which
surfaces as an opaque `{:stop, ...}`/exit rather than "you haven't started a Jido
instance."

## Why it matters

This is the very first thing a new user does. The failure mode is an `ArgumentError` deep
inside `Registry`, with no breadcrumb pointing at `use Jido, otp_app: ...` or
`Jido.start/0`. The mismatch between three different "defaults" (`Jido`, `Jido.Default`,
per-app `MyApp.Jido`) also confuses the mental model.

## Recommendation

One of:

1. **Make the default real**: have `Jido.Application` start a `Jido`-named instance
   (registry, agent supervisor, task supervisor, runtime store). Cheap, and makes every
   doc example true. Gate it behind `config :jido, start_default_instance: true` if
   library users object to extra processes.
2. **Fail fast and loud**: in `Options.new/1`, verify `Process.whereis(registry)` and
   return a `validation_error` like
   *"No Jido instance `Jido` is running â€” pass `jido: MyApp.Jido` or start one with
   `Jido.start/0`"*.
3. At minimum, unify the fallback name with `Jido.default_instance()` (`Jido.Default`) so
   `Jido.start()` actually makes the zero-config path work, and fix the moduledoc
   examples to pass `jido:`.
title: F04: The default `Jido` instance is referenced everywhere but never started
